prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, slower pacing with longer holds and restrained cuts.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. Faint light falls on an upturned face crowned with thorns against dark storm clouds; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
A close view of the crucified Christ's face turned upward, the mouth open around the cry, the eyes lifted toward a dark heaven with an anguished, searching appeal, a crown of thorns on the brow, faint light on the upturned face against deep shadow and storm clouds. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — the upturned crying face beneath the crown of thorns.

Cut to close-up — the open mouth around the cry.

Cut to insert — the upturned, searching eyes.

Cut to insert — the crown of thorns on the brow.

Cut to macro detail — the cords of the strained neck.

Cut to upper framing — the dark storm clouds above the face.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the upturned, crying face.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
Anguished, upward appeal.

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no halo added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image
